title: Diabetes technology documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact ADA device-principles section supports onset or duration of device use, interval change, functional impact, patient concerns, focused assessment, prior correspondence and questions about clinician-discussed diabetes technology without selecting a device.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact BGM section supports associated symptoms, relevant negatives and home-monitoring review when documented, including suspected inaccuracy or interference; it does not alter treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact CGM section supports skin findings, clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net and arranged follow-up only when applicable; it does not generate device or therapy instructions.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

sources_used.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
sources_used.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation

source_limitations: Device type, model, settings, wear, data period, accuracy, alarms, skin reaction, education, caregiver, insulin regimen, interpretation and plan remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
